nodes.label: AI as knowledge resource for unfamiliar domains
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for domain-specific complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I often use AI to help understand new technologies

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I collaborate, it makes me faster. I still don't trust it to do things by itself

nodes.label: AI reliability and hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: AI often just gets confused and makes up wrong data

nodes.label: Selective AI use based on task importance
nodes.label_negative: Open-ended tasks leading to unreliable output
nodes.grounding_span: I get it to do very specific things, smaller task items, smaller questions

nodes.label: AI as skill amplifier
nodes.label_negative: AI enabling mediocrity in poor workers
nodes.grounding_span: I feel it makes good people better, but bad people just pump out mid level code fast

nodes.label: AI use sanctioned by workplace
nodes.label_negative: Unsupervised or free AI use causing misinformation
nodes.grounding_span: All of them use it the same, I insist upon it as the person in charge

nodes.label: Productivity and efficient task completion
nodes.grounding_span: My job is to produce results, not to have fun

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: find out what unknowns I do not know I don't know yet

nodes.label: Job security and skill relevance
nodes.grounding_span: It just motivates me to stay on top so that I am not replaced

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for cross-domain learning
nodes.grounding_span: Successful it when it can help me with things like electrical design where I have much less experience

nodes.label: Frustration with AI tool failures
nodes.grounding_span: Frustrated is when my CEO will use free AI, like google search gemini and it gives him wrong info

nodes.label: Cautious trust in AI
nodes.grounding_span: I might trust it a bit more over time, but I think just making it enhance my workflow is best

nodes.label: Confidence in AI as a key skill
nodes.grounding_span: I think it will cut the bottom 25 or 50 percent and I think I am not in that group

nodes.label: Systematic iterative decomposition of tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I get it to do very specific things, smaller task items, smaller questions

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through back-and-forth questioning
nodes.grounding_span: After that I go back and forth with a few lines per prompt, maybe questioning its answers
